note: Cognee ECL vein NOT saturated; 1 cheap win + 2 feed the semantic-edge-layer initiative.

claim: NET-NEW (L/S, cheap win): JSON-driven entity-extraction patterns (10 built-in types, configurable regex+metadata) loadable without code changes vs our hard-coded 5 regex rules.

NET-NEW (M/M): two-tier persistent hierarchical summary (root world-summary + top-k subsections), prepended as grounding prelude BEFORE retrieved context. Our summary stage1 channel is flat; no hierarchical root+sub index, no pre-fetch-and-prepend.

NET-NEW (M/M), feeds semantic-edge-layer: edge relationship names in a dedicated vector collection (EdgeType_relationship_name), searched alongside nodes, contributing to triplet scoring via separate edge distances. Our causal edges are SQLite-only, no edge vector index, never inform ranking.

EXTENDS community-detection (H/L), feeds semantic-edge-layer: query across 5+ vector collections + edge-relation vectors as first-class targets; project subgraph; score triplets = node1 + edge + node2 effective distances + topology.

NET-NEW (M/M, NEEDS-VERIFY): on re-ingest, compare content hash and RESET pipeline_status -> re-extract/re-embed, vs simple dedup-skip. Our dedup.ts skips-if-unchanged but may not auto-reset derived artifacts. Verify vs the reindex path.
maps_to: handlers/save/dedup.ts (auto-reset derived artifacts on content change)

claim: EXTENDS CG-neighborhood-rescore-ranking (H/L, NEEDS-VERIFY): query-time k-hop BFS from vector-matched seeds, re-score expansion nodes via ID-filtered vector search. DeepSeek claimed no seam, but internal enableCausalBoost (2-hop) + banked neighborhood-rescore likely overlap — verify before net-new.
